behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all relevant files (todo.ts, registry.ts, session/todo.ts), the required literals (`export const defaultLayer`, `Todo.defaultLayer`, `yield* Todo.Service`, `Effect.gen(function* () { ... })`, `Tool.defineEffect`), and mentions tests in packages/opencode/test/.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the required pattern/outcome but doesn't provide the literal diff; developer still reasons about how to wire things up.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check semantic requirements (Effect.isEffect, exported defaultLayer, yield* Todo.Service, Effect.gen(function*)) which are all explicitly required by instruction, and alternative correct implementations following these rules would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute bun runtime check for Effect.isEffect(TodoWriteTool), run typecheck, and run multiple bun test suites, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_todowrite_is_effect actually imports and checks Effect.isEffect, and typecheck/repo tests would fail on stubs.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All required behaviors (Effect-based, yield service, exported defaultLayer, registry wiring, Effect.gen) are individually tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived) with descriptive names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task doesn't produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers appear consistent and correct across instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to look at but doesn't point to exact buggy line; localization still required within each file.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code task, not an agentmd-edits task.
